title: feature-context — lockfile for one open feature worktree (feat-003 git-agent)
description: Written by git-agent `checkout-feature`, updated by git-agent + every agent in the feature.agent_sequence. Lives at .claude/worktrees/{worktree}/.feature-context.json. Source of truth for worktree state — orchestrator reads it to route conflicts to the last writing agent, to detect stale worktrees on resume, and to decide when emergency-abort is safe. Never delete manually except as part of `just git-cleanup` housekeeping.

feature_id.description: Matches tasks.yaml features[].id exactly. Immutable after checkout-feature writes it.

worktree.description: Matches tasks.yaml features[].worktree; also the directory name under .claude/worktrees/.

branch.description: Matches tasks.yaml features[].branch. Created fresh from origin/main at checkout-feature time.

opened_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (Zulu) when git-agent checkout-feature wrote this lockfile.

opened_from.description: `<ref>@<sha>` string, e.g. `main@a1b2c3d4`. Records the commit the feature branch forked from.

agent_sequence.description: Verbatim copy of features[i].agent_sequence from tasks.yaml at checkout time. Mirrored here so git-agent operations don't require re-parsing tasks.yaml.

agent_history.description: Append-only log of agent invocations against this worktree. Each agent in agent_sequence writes one entry when it completes; git-agent adds resolve-conflict entries as they fire.

attempt.description: Retry attempt counter within this op. execute-tasks typically attempt=1 (unless task retries fire); resolve-conflict goes 1..3.

finished_at.description: null while the op is in progress; filled when the agent signals completion.

outcome.description: success → advance to next agent in sequence; failure → trigger per-task retry or emergency-abort per orchestrator policy; in-progress → transient state while the op runs.

commit_sha.description: If the agent committed work inside the worktree, the sha of that commit (or the HEAD sha after multiple commits). null if no commit.

notes.description: Free-form note (e.g. failure reason, retry context). Kept short — detailed logs go to orchestrator stdout.

last_writing_agent.description: The agent whose most-recent agent_history entry has outcome: success + non-null commit_sha. Used by close-feature to route merge conflicts back to the last editor. Derived from agent_history but cached here for O(1) lookup. null until the first agent completes successfully.

status.description: Worktree lifecycle state: open = actively being worked; merge-conflict = close-feature hit a conflict + awaiting resolve-conflict-handoff; closed = successfully merged, worktree dir removed (lockfile retained briefly for audit); aborted = emergency-abort fired, worktree forcibly removed.

conflict_files.description: When status: merge-conflict, list of conflicting file paths (relative to repo root). Populated by close-feature; consumed by resolve-conflict-handoff's return payload.

conflict_detected_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when close-feature first detected the conflict. Persists across resolve-conflict attempts until either close-feature succeeds (status → closed) or emergency-abort fires (status → aborted).

merge_sha.description: The sha of the `git merge --no-ff` commit on main. Populated when status transitions to closed. null otherwise.

failure_reason.description: When status: aborted, the orchestrator-supplied reason passed to emergency-abort. Mirrored into tasks.yaml features[i].failure_reason for human review.
